title: ostrya guatemalensis
intro: Ostrya guatemalensis, also known as the Guatemalan hophornbeam, is a deciduous tree species native to the tropical regions of Central America and Mexico. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ostrya guatemalensis include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Ostrya guatemalensis are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken to harness its medicinal properties.
